message:
perf(storage): bound recursive ready-query with CTE rather than IN expansion (beads_rust-dl86)

Replace the Rust-side BFS + unbounded IN(...) expansion for
`ready --parent <id> --recursive` with a single SQL recursive CTE:

  WITH RECURSIVE _descendants(did) AS (
    SELECT issue_id FROM dependencies
    WHERE depends_on_id = ? AND type = 'parent-child'
    UNION
    SELECT d.issue_id FROM dependencies d
    JOIN _descendants ON d.depends_on_id = _descendants.did
    WHERE d.type = 'parent-child'
  )
  SELECT ... FROM issues WHERE ... AND id IN (SELECT did FROM _descendants)

Before: collect_descendant_ids issued one query per parent node (O(depth)
round-trips), collected all descendant IDs into a Vec, then built
chunked IN(?,?,...) clauses with up to 900 placeholders each. For a
10k-node epic tree, this meant ~10k queries + a massive SQL string.

After: a single query with a recursive CTE that SQLite evaluates
entirely server-side. Zero Rust-side BFS, zero IN expansion, one
bound parameter.

The comment "not yet supported in fsqlite subqueries" was outdated —
fsqlite has had recursive CTE support with passing tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
